Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Your Transaction Team

Dear {{sellerName}},

Your ESOP transaction is managed by a team of independent professionals, each with a specific role. Here's who they are and what they do:

Transaction Steward
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
deals@forhemit.com
Coordinates the entire transaction — timeline, team, documents, gate clearances. Your single point of contact. Does not serve as a fiduciary, broker, or legal advisor.
Independent ESOP Trustee
{{trusteeName}}
{{trusteeFirm}}
Acts on behalf of the ESOP and its future participants. Engages the appraiser, determines FMV, reviews all transaction documents, and executes the purchase on behalf of the ESOP. Independent of Forhemit and the seller.
ERISA Counsel
{{counselName}}
{{counselFirm}}
Represents the ESOP trustee on legal matters. Reviews transaction structure for ERISA compliance, fiduciary duty, and prohibited transaction issues. Engaged by and reports to the trustee — not to Forhemit or the seller.
Independent Appraiser
{{appraiserName}}
{{appraiserFirm}}
Determines the Fair Market Value (FMV) of the company. Selected by the trustee. Provides the valuation report that sets the purchase price. ASA-accredited, ESOP-experienced.
SBA Lender
{{lenderName}}
{{lenderContact}}
Provides the SBA 7(a) financing for the transaction. The operating company is the borrower. Lender underwriting runs in parallel with the trustee's fiduciary review.
§ 02Who Does What
A.Forhemit coordinates. The professionals decide. Forhemit manages the process, but the trustee decides the price, the lender decides the terms, and counsel advises on the law. We don't override their independent judgment.
B.Each professional has a client. The trustee's client is the ESOP. Counsel's client is the trustee. The appraiser's client is the trustee. The lender's client is the operating company. Forhemit's client is you — but we serve as coordinator, not advisor.
C.Communication flows through Forhemit. To avoid confusion and duplication, most communication flows through Forhemit. If a team member needs to speak with you directly, we'll coordinate the introduction.
§ 03Your Attorney & CPA

You should also have your own personal advisors engaged:

1.Your attorney. Reviews the Engagement Letter, LOI, and Stock Purchase Agreement on your behalf. Advises on personal legal implications, estate planning, and post-close employment terms.
2.Your CPA. Advises on §1042 tax deferral, entity conversion implications, and personal tax planning. Coordinates with the appraiser on basis and timing issues.
Important: The deal team (trustee, counsel, appraiser) represents the ESOP — not you personally. Your personal attorney and CPA are your advocates. We strongly recommend you engage them before signing any binding documents.
§ 04Contact
{{stewardshipLeadName}}
deals@forhemit.com
